rubric_identity.one_sentence_purpose: Evaluate CVs for AI Architect roles by prioritizing enterprise AI platform design, production delivery, secure integration, and stakeholder translation over people management or academic research.
rubric_identity.core_persona: Senior individual contributor or architect-first player-coach who designs enterprise AI platforms, aligns stakeholders around architecture decisions, ships production systems with guardrails and evaluation, and stays close to implementation while translating technical tradeoffs for business stakeholders.
job_overlay_notes: Adjust cloud provider emphasis (Azure, AWS, GCP) only when JD explicitly requires provider-specific delivery evidence
Weight agents/orchestration higher when JD mentions agentic workflows, MCP, or multi-step automation
Increase guardrails/governance weight for roles in regulated industries or with explicit compliance mentions
Adjust consulting-style stakeholder signals for client-facing vs internal platform roles
Weight RAG and evaluation signals higher when JD emphasizes retrieval quality or production AI trust

what_good_looks_like: Headline uses architect-family titles (AI Solution Architect, AI Architect, Generative AI Architect) matching 87% category title distribution
Keywords 'solution architecture', 'platform design', 'enterprise architecture' appear 2-3 times across headline, summary, and experience
GenAI production keywords (agents, RAG, guardrails, evaluation) appear when backed by shipped work
Cloud provider names appear only when delivery on that provider is evidenced
No single technical keyword dominates since no hard skill exceeds 35.2% category frequency
common_failures: Headline inflates to Head of AI, Director, or VP titles when category is 79% senior IC
Overloads framework lists (LangChain, LangGraph, etc.) without tying to architecture outcomes
Claims multi-cloud authority when only one provider is evidenced
Uses research-oriented titles (AI Research Architect) when category has 0% PhD requirement
Omits key category keywords like 'platform design', 'enterprise integration', or 'guardrails'

what_to_adjust_per_jd: Mirror exact JD title if it matches safe title families (Solutions Architect, AI Architect)
Add provider-specific keywords (Azure AI, AWS Bedrock) only when JD requires and evidence exists
Emphasize agents/orchestration keywords when JD mentions agentic systems or workflow automation
Include specific framework names only when JD lists them and CV shows delivery
region_overrides: EU roles: include GDPR/EU AI Act keywords only if compliance delivery is evidenced
German market: do not claim German language capability without explicit evidence
Global/remote: emphasize async collaboration and distributed systems experience

9_10: Headline matches category title families with architecture and AI platform keywords
All major category signals (platform design, enterprise integration, guardrails, delivery) appear with appropriate frequency
Framework and provider mentions are precisely scoped to evidenced work
Zero title inflation or unsupported technology claims
7_8: Headline is appropriate but may miss one differentiating keyword
Most category signals present but one or two underrepresented
Minor framework over-listing that does not distort the profile
Cloud provider mentions are accurate but could be better positioned
5_6: Headline is generic architect title without AI or platform specificity
Several key category keywords (agents, guardrails, evaluation) missing despite evidence
Framework lists appear without connection to architecture outcomes
Provider claims are vague or weakly connected to delivery
0_4: Headline uses inflated titles (Director, VP, Head) unsupported by evidence
Missing core category keywords (platform design, enterprise architecture)
Research or academic framing for a delivery-focused category
Keyword stuffing with technologies not evidenced in experience

red_flags: Title inflation to executive levels when evidence shows IC/player-coach scope
Claiming AWS, Azure, or multi-cloud authority without provider-specific delivery evidence
Research-oriented keywords (publications, novel models) when category has 0% research_heavy signal
Generic AI/ML keywords without architecture specificity
Framework lists exceeding 5 items without outcome connections

what_good_looks_like: Architecture decisions tied to measurable production outcomes (users served, uptime, latency, cost)
Platform rollout scope quantified (business units, tenants, deployment frequency)
Integration complexity evidenced (systems connected, APIs designed, services bounded)
Modernization results quantified (incident reduction, deployment velocity, debt reduction)
Evaluation and guardrails outcomes measured (quality scores, error reduction, compliance pass rates)
common_failures: Architecture claims without production delivery evidence
Platform design described without scale, reliability, or adoption metrics
GenAI work (RAG, agents) mentioned without quality or efficiency outcomes
Stakeholder collaboration claimed without decision or delivery acceleration evidence
Cost, latency, or throughput improvements claimed without specific numbers

what_to_adjust_per_jd: Emphasize scale metrics for roles mentioning enterprise or large-scale deployment
Foreground cost efficiency for roles with explicit cloud optimization focus
Weight evaluation and quality metrics for roles emphasizing AI trust or reliability
Adjust stakeholder impact framing for consulting vs internal platform roles
company_domain_overrides: Media/entertainment: emphasize content delivery scale and user engagement metrics
Financial services: foreground reliability, security, and compliance outcomes
Consulting: weight client delivery acceleration and adoption metrics
region_overrides: EU: include compliance-related outcomes (audit success, regulatory approval) if evidenced
Global remote: emphasize async collaboration outcomes and distributed team delivery

9_10: Every architecture claim backed by quantified production outcome
Platform scope clear (users, tenants, services, deployment frequency)
GenAI delivery outcomes measured (quality scores, latency, cost, reliability)
Stakeholder translation tied to decision acceleration or delivery outcomes
Modernization results quantified with before/after metrics
7_8: Most architecture claims have quantified outcomes
Platform scope is clear but one or two metrics missing
GenAI outcomes present but could be more specific
Stakeholder impact evident but not fully quantified
5_6: Architecture claims present but many lack quantification
Platform scope vague or missing key metrics
GenAI work described without quality or efficiency outcomes
Stakeholder collaboration mentioned without delivery connection
0_4: Architecture claims without any production evidence
No quantified outcomes across platform, GenAI, or integration work
Responsibilities listed without impact or results
Metrics appear fabricated or inconsistent with role scope

red_flags: Metrics that are implausible for the described role scope
Production claims without evidence of shipped systems
Cost or performance improvements without baseline comparisons
Platform scale claims without user, tenant, or service counts
Evaluation or quality claims without measurable scoring

what_good_looks_like: Platform design and architecture ownership prominently featured (98.9% category signal)
Production delivery evidence for enterprise AI systems with scale proof
GenAI production stack signals present when evidenced: agents/orchestration (38.6%), guardrails (37.5%), RAG (28.4%), evaluation (20.5%)
Stakeholder translation and communication skills demonstrated (76.1% category signal)
Senior IC or player-coach scope without management inflation
common_failures: Missing platform design or architecture ownership as core signal
GenAI signals (agents, RAG, guardrails) absent despite category emphasis
Communication and stakeholder skills not evidenced despite 76.1% category frequency
Framing as pure manager when category is 79% senior IC
Research or academic positioning for a delivery-focused category

what_to_adjust_per_jd: Weight agents/orchestration higher when JD explicitly mentions agentic systems
Foreground guardrails/governance for roles with compliance or security emphasis
Adjust RAG and vector search prominence when JD emphasizes retrieval capabilities
Mirror specific framework requirements (LangChain, LangGraph) when JD lists them
Emphasize cloud provider experience only when JD specifies platform requirements
company_domain_overrides: Consulting firms: weight stakeholder translation and client-facing delivery higher
Product companies: emphasize platform ownership and production scale
Regulated industries: foreground governance, security, and compliance signals
region_overrides: German market: do not claim German language proficiency without evidence
EU: weight GDPR/EU AI Act only when JD requires and evidence exists
Remote-first: emphasize distributed collaboration and async architecture work

GenAI production signals match JD emphasis with evidence
Scope and seniority match JD expectations without inflation

Core category signals present but one or two underweighted

5_6: Some JD requirements covered but notable gaps
Core category signals present but not prominently featured
GenAI signals misaligned with JD emphasis
Scope framing needs adjustment to match JD expectations
0_4: Major JD requirements not covered by CV evidence
Core category signals (platform design, architecture) missing
GenAI production signals absent despite JD requirements
Scope or seniority mismatched (manager framing for IC role or vice versa)

red_flags: Claiming JD-required skills without delivery evidence
Framing as people manager when JD seeks senior IC architect
Missing platform design or architecture signals for architecture role
Cloud provider claims without provider-specific delivery
Research framing when JD emphasizes production delivery

what_good_looks_like: Architectural authority demonstrated through target-state design ownership and platform decisions
System ownership evidenced by end-to-end delivery from design through production
Stakeholder translation skill shown through business-technical tradeoff communication
Technical guidance and mentoring without inflating to people management
Governance and production integration ownership rather than C-suite or P&L framing

Claiming direct reports, hiring ownership, or org-building when evidence shows IC scope
Using VP, Director, or Head titles when category is 79% senior IC
Describing board presentations or P&L ownership for an architect role

what_to_adjust_per_jd: Emphasize cross-functional architecture leadership for roles mentioning enterprise-wide scope
Weight stakeholder alignment higher for consulting or client-facing architect roles
Adjust mentoring emphasis based on JD player-coach signals
Frame governance ownership for roles with explicit compliance requirements
company_domain_overrides: Consulting: emphasize client stakeholder translation and advisory positioning
Enterprise product: foreground platform ownership and cross-team architecture decisions
Startups: weight hands-on delivery authority over stakeholder breadth
region_overrides: German market: avoid implicit seniority inflation common in US CVs
Global remote: emphasize async decision-making and distributed architecture authority

End-to-end system ownership from architecture through production delivery
Stakeholder translation demonstrated with decision or delivery outcomes

Governance and security ownership framed as architectural responsibility

Stakeholder translation skills evident but not fully quantified

Stakeholder skills mentioned without evidence of translation impact

Framing as literal executive leader with C-suite, P&L, or boardroom language

red_flags: C-suite, boardroom, or P&L framing for a senior IC architect category
Direct reports or team size claims without management role evidence
Hiring ownership claims when category shows 2.3% hiring signal
Org-building claims when category shows 11.4% org_building signal

Cloud provider and framework claims backed by named delivery examples
GenAI capabilities (agents, RAG, evaluation) tied to shipped production systems

common_failures: Metrics fabricated or implausible for described role scope
Title inflation from architect to Director/VP/Head without evidence
Multi-cloud claims when only one provider is evidenced
GenAI capability claims without production delivery proof
Research or academic framing for a delivery-focused category

Check framework mentions are evidenced when JD lists specific requirements
Validate scale claims are plausible for company size and role scope

region_overrides: German market: verify language proficiency claims with explicit evidence

Metrics are plausible and internally consistent
Titles match scope without inflation
Provider and framework claims backed by named delivery
No research, publication, or PhD claims without evidence

Metrics are plausible but one or two lack specific context

red_flags: Unsourced metrics or numbers that cannot be traced to specific roles
Fabricated titles or title inflation beyond evidenced scope

DevOps or infrastructure ownership claims when evidence shows architecture collaboration

pass_criteria: Platform design or AI architecture ownership evidenced in at least one role
Production delivery of AI/ML or enterprise systems demonstrated
Communication or stakeholder alignment skill evidenced
Senior IC or player-coach scope clearly positioned
fail_conditions: No platform design or architecture ownership evidence
No production delivery evidence for AI or enterprise systems
Pure research or academic positioning without delivery
Pure people management framing without architecture signals

pass_criteria: All title claims match evidenced scope without inflation
Cloud provider claims backed by named delivery examples
GenAI capability claims tied to shipped production systems
Management claims (reports, hiring, org-building) supported by role evidence
Compliance claims (GDPR, EU AI Act) have delivery or audit proof
fail_conditions: Title inflation to Director/VP/Head without management evidence
Multi-cloud claims without multi-provider delivery evidence
Research or PhD framing without academic credentials
Direct reports claimed without people management role
Customer-facing claims without stakeholder interaction evidence

pass_criteria: Positioned as architect or senior IC rather than executive leader
Delivery emphasis over research or experimentation
Stakeholder translation skill evident without C-suite framing
Production systems focus rather than prototype or POC emphasis
fail_conditions: Positioned as C-suite executive, board-level, or P&L owner
Research-first framing for a delivery-focused category
Pure management positioning without architecture signals
Prototype or experimentation emphasis without production delivery

scoring_guidance.how_to_interpret_executive_presence: For AI Architect category, executive_presence measures architectural authority, system ownership, stakeholder translation skill, and technical leadership. It does NOT measure literal executive leadership, C-suite access, P&L ownership, or direct reports. High scores require demonstrated platform ownership, architecture decision authority, and business-technical translation — framed as senior IC or player-coach scope. Penalize any framing that inflates IC scope to executive leadership.
scoring_guidance.how_to_penalize_unsupported_claims: Deduct points proportionally to claim severity: minor inflation (1-2 points), significant unsupported scope (3-4 points), fabricated metrics or titles (5+ points). Any unsourced metric, PhD/publication claim without evidence, or multi-cloud claim without multi-provider delivery should trigger red flag review. Claims from unsafe_or_weak_framing list require explicit evidence to include.
scoring_guidance.how_to_handle_category_vs_job_tradeoffs: When JD requirements conflict with category patterns, prioritize JD for jd_alignment dimension but maintain category anchors for other dimensions. For example, if JD requires Azure expertise but category shows only 15.9% Azure, require Azure delivery evidence for full jd_alignment credit but do not penalize other dimensions. Never allow JD requirements to justify unsupported claims.

evidence_ledger.rubric_rule: Platform design is table-stakes: 98.9% of roles require it, architecture is top valued evidence type
Category is senior IC/player-coach (79% senior_ic, 38% player_coach) not people management (2.3% hiring, 2.3% performance management)
Title families skew to solutions_architect (50%) and ai_architect (37%); avoid Director/VP/Head inflation
GenAI production signals: agents/orchestration (38.6%), guardrails/governance (37.5%), RAG (28.4%), evaluation/quality (20.5%)
No research emphasis: requires_phd 0%, research_heavy 0% — delivery-focused category
Communication (76.1%) and stakeholder management (44.3%) are high-frequency soft-skill signals
Cloud providers Azure and AWS each appear in only 15.9% of roles; multi_cloud 29% — provider claims require delivery evidence
No single hard skill exceeds 35.2% (Python highest); avoid keyword lists without outcome connections
Baseline shows candidate strengths in platform architecture, RAG, guardrails, semantic caching; gaps in agent orchestration framing and player-coach visibility
Unsafe claims include: AWS/Azure AI services authority without evidence, DevOps ownership, customer-facing architecture without evidence, GDPR/EU AI Act ownership without audit proof
